title: BEM-930 unified protocol

Purpose: unified general protocol with both high-level architecture and full detail, after operator clarified that an appendix/report is insufficient.

- This is not a separate appendix and not an analytical-only report.
- This is a single common protocol with full detail.
- It includes top-level architecture and detailed implementation sections.
- Objects GD/DIR/WRK have passports, rules, functions, curators, contours, channels and DoD; they do not have prompts.
- Prompts exist only for LLM-executed roles, providers and operational handlers.

- Definition of working state.
- Object passports for GD/DIR/WRK.
- Elements and rule inheritance.
- Internal contours, static/dynamic/temporary testing and workspaces.

- Internal contour state machine: Curator -> Analyst -> Auditor -> Executor -> Auditor -> Curator with revision through Analyst.
- Provider model: GPT Analyst, Claude Auditor, Claude Executor, fallback GPT-Codex/Codex.
- Rules, experience and LLM role prompts.
- Prompt pack and operational contracts: curator, analyst, auditor, executor, GPT analyst provider, Claude auditor, Claude executor, Codex fallback, Telegram handler, dispatch consumer, managed channel consumer, report generator.
- Telegram, ChatGPT and existing Deno/GitHub mechanisms.
- SSOT registries and schemas.
- Claude requirements as release blockers.
- Detailed roadmap steps 1.1..8.2 with files, acceptance criteria, proof and gates.
- Operator gates and release criteria.
- QA matrix of things not to lose.
